question_text: Explain why amplitude and loudness are related but why changing amplitude does not change the frequency of a sound wave.
correct_answer: Amplitude determines loudness—a larger amplitude means particles vibrate more, creating louder sound. Frequency determines pitch and depends on how fast the source vibrates. These are independent: you can have a loud high note or a quiet high note—both have the same frequency but different amplitudes.
explanation: Amplitude is the maximum displacement of particles from their rest position. Loudness depends on amplitude: bigger vibrations = louder sound. Frequency is how many vibrations occur per second. You can increase amplitude without changing frequency, just like shaking something more vigorously without shaking it faster.
